governance_action_profile.registered_action_ids_rule: the actions object keys are exactly action_order followed by failure_suffix_order; each action's success_sequence is its fixed position on the full pass path or null for a failure-only action; receipt.sequence is always the next positive integer in the actual verified trace and is never taken from success_sequence
governance_action_profile.argument_rule: init_round is accepted only by the distinct init-round operation; actions 2 through 24 and the two conditionally selected failure-suffix actions are accepted only by run --action; only result_review_binding and final_seal_audit_binding accept one artifact-ref and it must equal the action's exact round-specific template; bind_scoped_repair and close_round read only their fixed round-local inputs; no other option, positional argument, abbreviation, repeated argument, or action not selected by failure_state_machine is accepted

dispatch_depth.guard_stage: before round-root creation, mutation, log creation, handler invocation, child launch, or receipt allocation
dispatch_depth.reentry_rule: any present caller value or any direct or indirect governance re-entry fails at guard_stage and creates no receipt

derived_environment.derivation_rule: after validated no-symlink round-root and exact action selection, governance alone derives ACTION, RR, and dispatch depth

child_environment.directory_rule: init_round creates the no-symlink HOME and TMPDIR below RR before any subprocess action; later actions reject a missing, symlinked, or non-directory component
child_environment.digest_rule: child_environment_sha256 is SHA-256 of P01-canonical JSON bytes of the exact fully substituted child environment object
child_environment.parser_exception: parser subprocesses started by parser_fidelity_tests replace this environment with parser_fidelity_profile.environment and have their own exact invocation ledger

governance_action_profile.receipt_execution_rule: subprocess requires null handler_id plus non-null child_argv and child_environment_sha256; governance_native requires exact non-null handler_id and requires both child_argv and child_environment_sha256 to be null; init_round follows governance_native nullability; external_argv is always populated and must not equal a non-null child_argv
governance_action_profile.native_handler_rule: invoke the named handler exactly once in the current process; a native handler never invokes p02_governance.py, allocates a nested receipt, or launches any subprocess; the outer initializer or dispatcher alone writes the action receipt after the handler returns
governance_action_profile.subprocess_rule: launch exactly one direct child with shell false, fixed cwd at workspace root, exact child argv, exact child environment, captured stdout and stderr, no retry, and no alternate executable

entry_snapshot_schema.creation_rule: after the exact agreeing R9 plan review and before any implementation edit, require the Phase 02 phase root, entry root, and all four targets to be absent, then create the phase root, entry root, and all four files with no-overwrite writes; any pre-existing phase root, entry root, or target ref is preserved as partial or conflicting state and fails entry creation without retry; R10 and later are outside the human-authorized review budget and forbidden
entry_snapshot_schema.manifest_line_rule: lower-case SHA-256, two ASCII spaces, normalized workspace-relative ref, LF; records sorted by UTF-8 ref bytes with no duplicate ref, symlink, directory, cache, pyc, or pyo entry
entry_snapshot_schema.implementation_manifest_scope: every regular non-symlink non-cache file below src, tests, and scripts
entry_snapshot_schema.immutable_input_manifest_scope: compact oracle, materialized oracle, every parser_fidelity_profile.source_allowlist fixture/frozen source, with duplicate refs emitted once
entry_snapshot_schema.protected_manifest_scope: all pre-existing dirty files outside the reviewed implementation allowlist plus sealed P00/P01 records, mission/master/runbook/ledger/reset/anti-drift plans, every prior P02 plan-review bundle/result, and the reviewed plan itself; compact/materialized oracles and immutable sources are additionally bound by immutable_input_manifest
entry_snapshot_schema.agreeing_plan_review_grammar: strict UTF-8, LF only, no BOM/NUL/CR, at most 131072 bytes; each required line occurs exactly once as a complete line; VERDICT is the final nonempty line; no other VERDICT line exists; each digest is recomputed from the exact entry-bound file
entry_snapshot_schema.init_round_rule: accept no entry/review/manifest override argument; read only fixed_ref, strict-load and canonical-byte-check it, reopen every bound ref no-follow, verify all manifest lines and agreeing review grammar, then apply predecessor checks before RR creation

entry_bootstrap_profile.role: one-time supervisor bootstrap after plan AGREE and before implementation; not a production action or phase evidence
entry_bootstrap_profile.source_binding_rule: the final-budget R9 review bundle and agreeing review each bind the exact source SHA-256; the entry record copies that ref/digest after recomputing both source and agreeing-review lines; the digest is not embedded recursively in the compact oracle
entry_bootstrap_profile.implementation: reviewed standalone Python imports only EvidenceValidationError, canonical_json_bytes, content_digest, validate_logical_path, read_bytes_no_follow, and atomic_write_bytes_no_replace from sealed P01 evidence_manifest; it implements no extraction, parser, backend, or proof logic; it requires exact sys.orig_argv, exact six-key process environment, no bytecode writes, and no site initialization, and rejects source/profile disagreement for external argv, Git argv, child environment, or write order before mkdir; it derives the exact 13 unique source refs/digests from fixtures and frozen_sources, requires set equality with parser_fidelity_profile.source_allowlist, strict-decodes each source, applies environment_locator.comment_rule, pairs every supported begin/end token by exact name and rejects crossed/unclosed stacks, reconstructs source-derived ancestry, requires the declared ordered outer environment_span plus inherited nested_environment_span when present to equal that ancestry, verifies label token/value and all label/owned/excluded spans against the selected final environment, rehashes every owned span, strict-loads all 17 materialized records, verifies their canonical byte counts/digests/ids and exact compact-path set, reconstructs canonical environment stack descriptors/ids and selected descriptor/span/line bounds from source, requires exact equality with each materialized identity environment plus document source ref/digest and label, and creates the phase/entry directories through no-follow directory descriptors
entry_bootstrap_profile.only_parameter: AGREEING_REVIEW_REF matching entry_snapshot_schema.agreeing_plan_review_path_regex; argparse abbreviation, repeated option, positional values, and every other option are rejected
entry_bootstrap_profile.dirty_path_rule: parse porcelain v1 -z exactly, including rename two-path records; every returned normalized workspace-relative path outside the implementation allowlist enters protected_entry_manifest, while every protected plan/record required by entry_snapshot_schema.protected_manifest_scope is added even when clean
entry_bootstrap_profile.protected_review_ref_rule: add every regular no-follow file directly below docs/reviews whose filename starts mathdevmcp-real-document-remediation-phase-00-, phase-01-, or phase-02-plan-review- and ends -2026-07-11.md; reject symlinks and special files
entry_bootstrap_profile.precondition: the workspace root has real non-symlink .git, src, src/mathdevmcp, tests, scripts, docs, docs/reviews, .local, .local/mathdevmcp, and .local/mathdevmcp/evidence directories; formal Phase 02 phase root, entry root, and every target path are absent; reject any pre-existing phase root before loading the compact oracle or reading snapshot inputs; compact-oracle bootstrap source_ref matches the running script; the agreeing R9 review binds current plan, compact oracle, materialized oracle, and running bootstrap-source digests; all 13 unique reviewed sources equal the parser source allowlist and their strict UTF-8, comment-masked supported-environment pairing, exact outer-to-selected environment chain including inherited nested_environment_span, descriptor, label token/value, selected-environment containment, owned, excluded, and owned-span-digest projections verify; all 17 materialized records have exact canonical identities/path coverage and their document source/label plus reconstructed environment stacks, ids, selected descriptor/span, and line bounds equal the source-derived compact projections; every input is regular, no-follow, within workspace trust root, and verified before no-follow mkdir traversal
entry_bootstrap_profile.postcondition: require the Phase 02 phase root to contain exactly one regular non-symlink entry directory and that directory to contain exactly the four expected regular non-symlink outputs; reopen and byte-verify all four no-follow files, strictly parse each reopened manifest, re-enumerate each complete implementation/protected/immutable scope, reopen every current input no-follow, reconstruct each manifest from the current membership and bytes, and require byte equality with the reopened written manifest; the four exact bootstrap outputs are excluded from the post-write dirty-path set because they were absent at entry capture and are verified separately, while any other new phase-root or dirty path fails; this detects both record-byte and scope-membership drift; print only canonical JSON containing entry_record_ref and its recomputed sha256
entry_bootstrap_profile.failure_rule: before the first mkdir, fail with no write; after the Phase 02 phase-root mkdir or any later mkdir/no-replace/reopen/reconstruction failure, preserve all partial directories and bytes, perform no deletion/overwrite/retry, and require human recovery direction; a later invocation rejects the preserved phase root before snapshot reads

human_result_profile.encoding_rule: strict UTF-8, LF only, no BOM/NUL/CR, at most 262144 bytes
human_result_profile.footer_rule: each footer line occurs exactly once as a complete line in the declared order and they are the final four nonempty lines; RESULT_ROUND equals the round root; SHA256 equals the verified receipt-index head immediately before bind_result; DECISION is candidate_pass_pending_independent_result_review exactly when all reached required checks through diff have zero exit and every reconstructed criterion passes, otherwise blocked; prose outside the footer is narrative only

phase_result_schema.decision_rule: candidate_pass_pending_independent_result_review only when every required action through diff exists with zero exit, every required raw artifact reopens, all primary_criterion values including all_pass are true, and all vetoes are false; otherwise blocked
phase_result_schema.artifact_pair_rule: bundle/parser/mutation refs and digests are both non-null only after their generating action succeeded and otherwise both null; no failed or absent artifact is represented by a fabricated empty record
phase_result_schema.count_rule: backend_request_count and source_edit_count are independently recomputed nonnegative integers and are both zero for a candidate decision
phase_result_schema.primary_criterion_rule: exact boolean map in primary_criterion_keys order; all_pass is the conjunction of the preceding twelve and is never accepted from the Markdown result
phase_result_schema.veto_rule: exact boolean map independently reconstructed from round_close_schema.veto_diagnostic_sources; candidate decision requires all false

failure_state_machine.receipt_measurement_rule: every invoked action appends exactly one receipt/index even for nonzero underlying exit; dispatcher success reports durable measurement only; a receipt/index write or immediate-verification failure creates no trusted successor
failure_state_machine.init_round_failure_rule: all path, entry, predecessor, reviewed-binding, and manifest validation occurs before creating RR; a pre-allocation failure creates no RR and no receipt and may be rerun only after correcting the external precondition; once RR is created, failure to create and verify its init_round receipt/index is an unrecoverable governance-chain stop for that round root
failure_state_machine.first_failure_rule: the first nonzero receipt fixes the measured-failure trigger; no later success-path action is accepted except the local-check evidence suffix below
failure_state_machine.local_check_suffix_rule: after the first failed local check, stop all remaining checks; accept bind_result, then build_run_manifest only after zero-exit bind_result, then bind_scoped_repair; a nonzero bind_result or build_run_manifest skips directly to bind_scoped_repair
failure_state_machine.later_failure_rule: a nonzero construction_or_gate action accepts only bind_scoped_repair, except stable_publication with an existing stable path is an out-of-chain human-recovery stop
failure_state_machine.review_revise_rule: a zero-exit result_review_binding with strictly parsed REVISE or zero-exit final_seal_audit_binding with strictly parsed REVISE accepts only bind_scoped_repair; the triggering review or audit remains a reached non-null stage
failure_state_machine.transition_precedence: apply post-link stable-path and receipt/index failure rows first, then prior-local-failure suffix rows, then exact current-action review/audit/publication rows, then ordinary exit outcome; exactly one row must match or governance rejects the chain
failure_state_machine.post_link_rule: if the stable path exists after any publication or receipt/index failure, preserve it, claim no pass, append no repair or close receipt, forbid retry, and require human recovery
failure_state_machine.chain_failure_rule: a nonzero bind_scoped_repair or close_round receipt, or inability to append/verify any receipt/index, has no accepted successor and requires human recovery

scoped_repair_schema.failed_actions_rule: for measured_action_failure, exact receipt order of every nonzero action and nonempty; for either revise reason, exactly empty because the binding action succeeded and measured a REVISE verdict
scoped_repair_schema.source_artifact_rule: first failed canonical receipt for measured_action_failure; exact bound result-review file for result_review_revise; exact bound final-seal-audit file for final_seal_audit_revise
scoped_repair_schema.source_receipt_index_rule: the immutable receipt-index snapshot whose head is the triggering failed receipt, result_review_binding receipt, or final_seal_audit_binding receipt respectively
scoped_repair_schema.affected_paths_rule: sorted unique workspace-relative paths, each in the reviewed implementation allowlist; a required plan/oracle/source or other protected-path edit cannot be represented and stops for a newly reviewed entry contract
scoped_repair_schema.required_check_ids_rule: ordered unique nonempty subset of action_order positions 2 through 23; a repair may require the exact failed construction, candidate, review-binding, or final-audit-binding action in the next full round, while init_round and stable_publication are never repair-validation claims
scoped_repair_schema.non_claim_literal: A scoped repair requirement is not evidence that the finding is resolved.
scoped_repair_schema.repairs_rule: nonempty ordered list with unique finding_id

round_close_schema.stage_pair_rule: human-result and machine-result pairs become non-null together only after zero-exit bind_result; every pair is both null before the corresponding successful receipt and both non-null after it; once a pair is null every later stage pair is null, except a failed candidate/final gate may have its input candidate pair non-null while its validation-log pair is null; verified REVISE review/audit pairs remain non-null
round_close_schema.verdict_rule: result_review_verdict is null exactly when result_review_ref is null and otherwise AGREE or REVISE; final_seal_audit_verdict is null exactly when final_seal_audit_ref is null and otherwise AGREE or REVISE
round_close_schema.predecessor_rule: all four predecessor close/index fields are null for rr01 and non-null for rr02 through rr05, naming the immediately previous round's canonical close and terminal close-round receipt-index
round_close_schema.log_inventory_rule: ordered by receipt sequence then stdout before stderr; includes every reached receipt log before close; exit_code equals that receipt's measured exit

veto_diagnostic_sources.predecessor_binding_failure: init_round or protected_check independently fails exact P01 or prior-P02 close/index reconstruction
veto_diagnostic_sources.frozen_source_digest_failure: immutable-input comparison reports either frozen document source digest changed
veto_diagnostic_sources.row_span_or_roundtrip_failure: localizer or extraction summaries report any reviewed environment, row, label, owned, excluded, or source envelope span mismatch
veto_diagnostic_sources.label_or_file_ownership_failure: extraction summaries report an implicit label, wrong file, duplicate lookup selection, missing exact-file lookup, or owned-row mismatch
veto_diagnostic_sources.continuation_or_ambiguity_failure: extraction summaries report a grouping transition, reason, ambiguity code, orphan state, overlap allocation, or uncertainty mismatch
veto_diagnostic_sources.duplicate_or_drifting_identity: complete-obligation reconstruction reports duplicate ids or any canonical byte-count, digest, id, environment-id, or row-id mismatch
veto_diagnostic_sources.cross_label_contamination: scoped source, excluded span, normalized target, or inventory comparison reports sibling-label content
veto_diagnostic_sources.incomplete_target_routed: integration ledger reports any non-valid, ambiguous, orphaned, incomplete, fallback, or manufactured target entered an adapter-capable route
veto_diagnostic_sources.parser_fidelity_or_provenance_failure: parser profile or comparison reports executable/version/argv/environment/source/output mismatch, timeout/nonzero/malformed result, ownership disagreement, or unjustified selection
veto_diagnostic_sources.artifact_or_bundle_integrity_failure: any measured action has nonzero exit, or any required artifact/ref/digest/schema/reopen comparison fails
veto_diagnostic_sources.oracle_materialization_failure: compact/materialized/source reconstruction, scanner, normalizer, or identity mutation summary reports mismatch
veto_diagnostic_sources.independent_review_not_agreed: strict result-review or final-seal-audit verdict is REVISE
veto_diagnostic_sources.backend_execution_detected: independent total of forbidden-attempt ledger entries is nonzero, including blocked attempts
veto_diagnostic_sources.source_edit_detected: immutable-input entry/exit comparison reports any fixture, oracle, or frozen-source byte change
veto_diagnostic_sources.publication_quarantine_failure: quarantine tests or reconstructed result report publication enabled/eligible or extraction bypass
veto_diagnostic_sources.unexpected_implementation_path: entry/exit implementation delta contains a path outside the reviewed allowlist
veto_diagnostic_sources.protected_baseline_drift: protected entry/exit comparison reports any unrelated dirty, sealed P00/P01, or protected path changed
veto_diagnostic_sources.governance_chain_failure: receipt/index append or verification, bind_scoped_repair, close_round, or post-link sealing fails; this veto cannot appear in a trusted canonical close because no trusted close successor exists

round_close_schema.veto_rule: exact boolean map in veto_keys order reconstructed only from veto_diagnostic_sources; measured_action_failure always sets artifact_or_bundle_integrity_failure true and sets every other source whose exact diagnostic is observed; either revise reason sets independent_review_not_agreed true; at least one veto is true; unobserved vetoes are false; governance_chain_failure is false because a chain failure cannot produce a trusted canonical close

governance_action_profile.successor_round_rule: rr01 has null predecessor fields; rr02 through rr05 require immediately prior rrNN round-close and terminal receipt-index; terminal index must end in zero-exit close_round whose bindings recompute the supplied close digest and preceding bind_scoped_repair index; the close must bind that repair receipt head; skipped ids, success-path indexes, nonterminal indexes, and any digest mismatch are rejected
governance_action_profile.receipt_binding_rule: receipt bindings have exactly the keys listed for check_id; on a handler exception before a binding exists each listed value is null and exit is nonzero; close_round and stable_publication require every listed value non-null, with same_inode and same_digest true for stable_publication

environment_locator.comment_rule: mask from each unescaped percent sign through but not including LF; percent is unescaped exactly when immediately preceded by an even count of consecutive backslashes
environment_locator.brace_rule: environment tokens are recognized only outside masked comments; begin/end tokens are paired by exact captured name using a stack and unbalanced or crossed pairs are invalid
environment_locator.environment_span_rule: half-open bytes from the first backslash byte of begin through the closing brace byte of the matching end; a following LF is excluded
environment_locator.nesting_rule: retain every supported nested environment; an obligation selects the innermost environment containing all its owned spans and records every supported ancestor outermost first
environment_locator.row_content_rule: within the selected environment, exclude its begin/end token bytes and split the remaining bytes at row separators using row_classifier.row_separator_rule; child-environment begin/end token bytes are structural and excluded from child row content
environment_locator.row_trim_rule: remove only leading and trailing ASCII bytes space, tab, CR, LF, form feed, and vertical tab from each split part; never trim internal bytes
environment_locator.empty_row_rule: a trimmed empty split part is a blank-math-row stop and is not an owned row
environment_locator.line_rule: line_start or line_end equals one plus the count of LF bytes strictly before the corresponding half-open byte offset
environment_locator.failure_rule: invalid UTF-8, unbalanced/crossed supported environments, invalid brace depth, or an owned span outside its selected environment fails closed as invalid

row_classifier.input_scope: one byte-preserving row after top-level separator splitting and outer ASCII-whitespace trimming
row_classifier.comment_rule: from each unescaped percent sign through LF is masked for classification; percent is unescaped exactly when immediately preceded by an even count of consecutive backslashes
row_classifier.top_level_rule: brace depth zero in the currently scanned environment and outside every child environment nested inside it; when scanning the inner aligned environment its own content is top level
row_classifier.row_separator_rule: two consecutive backslash bytes at top level outside a masked comment; separator bytes are not part of either row
row_classifier.had_row_terminator_rule: true exactly when this row ended at a recognized row separator
grammar_removals: all ampersand bytes at top level
row_classifier.terminal_punctuation_rule: remove at most one terminal comma or period after ASCII-whitespace collapse

classification_precedence.when: invalid braces or nesting, any top-level relation other than equality or definition, both equality and definition, or relation_count != 1 when any relation exists
one leading equality, empty lhs, and nonempty rhs
no top-level relation and continuation_prefix_regex matches at offset zero
one equality with nonempty lhs/rhs and has_number_suppressor and had_row_terminator
one definition with nonempty lhs/rhs and has_number_suppressor and had_row_terminator

transitions.accepted_sequence: zero or more immediately following unlabeled relation_chain_continuation rows in the same non-nested environment stack
one or more immediately following unlabeled relation_continuation rows
one or more immediately following unlabeled relation_continuation rows
zero or more immediately preceding unlabeled relation_continuation rows then exactly one immediately preceding unlabeled relation_head or definition_head

grouping_grammar.nested_aligned_exception: exactly one immediately preceding unlabeled complete_relation with the same exact outer equation and inner aligned stack and only row-separator bytes plus ASCII whitespace in the intervening source
stop_before: any explicit label not equal to the candidate seed
any shape not named by the active transition
grouping_grammar.scan_policy: maximal contiguous scan; never skip a row; never cross an environment boundary; classify once; no iterative reclassification
grouping_grammar.commit_policy: compute every candidate before commitment; commit disjoint sets in source order; every overlapping set becomes ambiguous with code competing_owner_sets; no distance or first-wins tie-breaker

reason_assignment.backward_number_suppressed_relation_head: an unlabeled relation_head or definition_head attached by a backward transition
reason_assignment.forward_relation_continuation: an unlabeled relation_chain_continuation or relation_continuation attached by a forward transition
reason_assignment.excluded_sibling_label: a disjoint row owned by another valid label in the same source/environment
reason_assignment.orphan_no_unique_owner: a seed for which no complete candidate exists
reason_assignment.ambiguous_competing_owner: either two or more explicit labels compete for the same classified row before allocation, or two or more computed candidate owner sets overlap after allocation; the ambiguity code distinguishes multiple_explicit_labels_on_one_row from competing_owner_sets
reason_assignment.ambiguous_parser_disagreement: the parser fidelity route disagrees on ownership or exact byte mapping

target_normalizer.input_rule: process each owned raw row independently in source order; never read excluded spans
row_transform_order: remove a terminal top-level row separator when present
collapse each maximal row_classifier.ascii_whitespace_regex match to one ASCII space
strip leading and trailing ASCII space
remove at most one terminal comma or period and strip trailing ASCII space again
target_normalizer.token_preservation: preserve all other bytes exactly after UTF-8 decoding and NFC normalization; do not expand macros, normalize command spelling, insert operator spacing, simplify algebra, or reorder terms

composition_by_owned_row_shapes.rule: the transformed row is display_text and its one top-level equality split gives two members
the transformed row is display_text and its one top-level definition split gives two members
append one ASCII space plus each transformed continuation row to the transformed head in source order; split the result at its one top-level equality
append one ASCII space plus each transformed continuation row to the transformed head in source order; split the result at its one top-level definition
append one ASCII space plus each transformed chain row to the transformed first row in source order; split at every top-level equality in source order
display_text is empty, members is empty, and complete_lhs_rhs is false

target_normalizer.top_level_split_rule: use row_classifier.top_level_rule and longest-first relation tokens; delimiter bytes are omitted and each member is stripped of leading/trailing ASCII space
target_normalizer.validity_rule: the exact owned shape sequence must match one composition entry, the declared delimiter type/count must match, and every member must be nonempty; otherwise normalized_target is unavailable and the record cannot be adapter eligible
target_normalizer.display_text_rule: join the stripped members with one ASCII-space-delimiter-space, using equals for equality/equality_chain and backslash-coloneqq for aligned_definition

parser_fidelity_profile.scope: differential extraction validation only; never mathematical backend evidence
parser_fidelity_profile.case_token_rule: CASE is the lower-case SHA-256 of the workspace-relative SOURCE string
parser_fidelity_profile.round_root_rule: RR is the validated no-symlink result-round root; every parser output, log, temporary directory, cache, and HOME is below RR/parser
parser_fidelity_profile.invocation_rule: run each version argv exactly once and each fidelity argv exactly once per source; no shell, stdin, directory recursion, glob, backend_bin lookup, executable override, extra argv, retry, or source outside source_allowlist
parser_fidelity_profile.artifact_rule: capture immutable stdout, stderr, exit code, timeout state, exact argv/environment, output/log bytes when applicable, and SHA-256 of SOURCE before and after invocation; source mutation is a veto
parser_fidelity_profile.field_scoring: each field is 1 only for exact compact-oracle equality, 0 otherwise; unavailable or not source-mappable is 0
parser_fidelity_profile.selection_rule: compare seven-bit vectors lexicographically in priority order; a specialist is materially better only when it has 1 at the first differing field where current has 0 and every higher-priority field is equal; equal vectors retain current; no scalar sum, label count, parse success, output size, or runtime tie-breaker
parser_fidelity_profile.veto_rule: any specialist/current ownership disagreement, non-source-mappable output, malformed output, timeout, nonzero exit, executable/version mismatch, argv/environment mismatch, missing raw artifact, or mutation of a source makes the reviewed case inconclusive or ambiguous and cannot promote a parser
parser_fidelity_profile.network_and_backend_boundary: no network, filters, Lua, PDF engines, shell escape, bibliography execution, mathematical adapter, proof backend, or recursive corpus scan is authorized

obligation_materialization.source_ref_field: nearest source_ref inherited from the item, then its containing case
obligation_materialization.source_digest_field: nearest source_sha256 inherited from the item, then its containing case; duplicate-file items resolve against the parallel containing source_refs/source_sha256s entry

document_logical_id.fixture_rule: p02-fixture/ plus the POSIX source filename stem
document_logical_id.frozen_rule: p02-frozen/ plus the lower-case source SHA-256

obligation_materialization.environment_spans: ordered outer environment_span followed by nested_environment_span when present; duplicate-file items own their environment_span; the selected environment is the final span
obligation_materialization.environment_kind_rule: captured name with one terminal star removed
obligation_materialization.environment_starred_rule: true exactly when the captured name has a terminal star
obligation_materialization.environment_id_rule: env_ plus SHA-256 of P01-canonical environment_identity_payload for each outer-to-current stack prefix
obligation_materialization.parser_field_boundary: for these 17 reviewed obligations the two canonical parser values are stable obligation-schema tokens; LaTeXML/Pandoc discovery, comparison, version, and disagreement records live outside their identity and may veto but never rewrite them; materially better specialist fidelity requires a reviewed compact/full oracle amendment before result rerun; a new non-frozen input may use a specialist selected by the reviewed fidelity rubric and must bind that selected backend/version in its new identity
obligation_materialization.line_start_rule: 1 plus the count of LF bytes in source_bytes[0:start_byte]
obligation_materialization.line_end_rule: 1 plus the count of LF bytes in source_bytes[0:end_byte]
obligation_materialization.row_universe_rule: row_index is the zero-based source-order position among every nonempty localized math row in the selected environment; for every reviewed valid case the sorted unique union of owned_spans plus excluded_spans must equal that complete localized row universe, allowing independent oracle materialization without implementation output
obligation_materialization.owned_row_rule: when owned_spans is nonempty, owned_spans, owned_span_sha256s, row_shapes, and grouping_reasons must have equal length and zip in source order; when owned_spans is empty, owned_rows is empty and row_shapes/grouping_reasons are oracle-only classifier diagnostics whose source is bound by ambiguities; explicit_label and label_span are the owner label/span exactly when the label span is contained in an owned row, otherwise null; has_nonumber is true exactly when comment-masked row bytes contain command token \\nonumber or \\notag; environment_stack is the ordered environment-id list
obligation_materialization.row_id_rule: row_ plus SHA-256 of P01-canonical row_identity_payload
obligation_materialization.owned_span_rule: map each owned interval to exact start_byte, end_byte, line_start, and line_end
obligation_materialization.continuation_span_rule: the owned span at each position whose grouping_reason is not explicit_label_seed, preserving source order
obligation_materialization.excluded_span_rule: map each excluded interval to its line span, excluded_sibling_label equal to the unique other valid expected obligation in the same source/environment whose owned_spans contains that exact interval, and reason excluded_sibling_label; zero or multiple matches invalidates the oracle
obligation_materialization.source_math_rule: empty string when owned_spans is empty; otherwise UTF-8 decode the exact source byte envelope from the first owned start_byte through the last owned end_byte, including unchanged intervening row separators and whitespace; any excluded span overlapping that envelope invalidates materialization
obligation_materialization.provenance_rule: ordered file ref with source SHA-256, selected environment ref/span, owner-label ref/span, then one ref/span for each owned row
obligation_materialization.identity_payload_rule: strict schema_version 1.0 record materialized by these rules, excluding derived obligation_id and obligation_digest and excluding oracle-only projection/hash fields
obligation_materialization.bare_duplicate_lookup_boundary: expected_lookup ambiguity is a lookup result outside LabelScopedObligation identity; each file_scoped_obligation is materialized independently

ambiguities.required_discriminator: retain exactly one explicit owning label or split the mathematical row into separately labeled rows
retain exactly one explicit owning label or split the mathematical row into separately labeled rows
supply the exact workspace-relative source file together with label eq:duplicate
supply one adjacent number-suppressed relation head in the same environment stack

normalized_target.display_text: \Delta CF_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s) = \Delta PPNR_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s) - \Delta EL_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s) - \Delta Kchg_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s) - \Delta Tax_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s) + \Delta RelValue_{i,t+h}(a,\pi;s)

identity_mutation_protocol.coverage_rule: every must-change path is replaced independently in a fresh copy of identity_payload; every must-not-change path is replaced independently in a fresh copy of non_identity_test_envelope while identity_payload remains byte-identical
